title: gilia peninsularis
intro: Gilia peninsularis, also known as the Peninsular gilia or Bluefield gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gilia peninsularis include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Gilia peninsularis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
